rows.requirement: Seed a small corpus (>=3 real distinct-content files)
rows.test: test_query_e2e.py::_seed_corpus fixture writes 3 real markdown files with genuinely distinct content under tmp_path

rows.requirement: Run a real query through the full pipeline (intent_refiner -> topic_selector -> synthesizer)
rows.test: Both tests call run_query_pipeline() directly with no pipeline-internal step mocked; only the LLMClient and the injected search_candidates/graph_neighbors/get_file DI-seam callables are fakes (per 4.6.1's disclosed DI seam)

rows.requirement: Synthesized answer's valid citation resolves to a real file in the seeded corpus
rows.test: assert result.synthesis.citations == [<real seeded path>] and result.synthesis.unknown_citations() == []

rows.requirement: No hallucinated citations pass silently -- a citation to a file NOT in the seeded corpus is flagged
rows.test: assert result.synthesis.unknown_citations() == ['made/up/NotInCorpus.md'] while the real seeded path is NOT in unknown_citations()

rows.requirement: get_file/search_candidates are backed by real on-disk content, not a hardcoded fixture dict
rows.test: _make_get_file/_make_search_candidates read (tmp_path / path).read_text() on every call; assert real file-header text appears in the synthesis prompt sent to the fake LLM
